blocks.content: At seven o'clock Davenport called again.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: The bracket. There are two fibers caught in the paint scrape. Synthetic. Dark colored. Consistent with athletic clothing.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Lab confirmation?

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Takes time. But it's enough for me to reclassify formally. This is a homicide investigation as of tonight.
Julian let out a slow breath.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The house booking.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Working on it. The reservation was made through an intermediary — one member of the group suggested it but another one handled the actual booking.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Which one handled it.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That's an active part of the investigation, Mr. Crane.
Julian almost smiled. Again.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: I will say this — whoever handled the booking viewed the property listing seventeen times before making the reservation. Including the exterior photo gallery. Three times.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: They were looking at the window.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That's one interpretation.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: It's the right one.
Davenport was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: I'll be at the house tomorrow morning. Eight o'clock.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I'll be next door.

He hung up. He sat in the dark study for a moment. The ocean was steady outside. Next door the lights were on — all of them, every room lit up as though the five people inside needed the brightness to hold off something that couldn't be held off.
One of them had booked that house. Had looked at that window. Had counted on a locked door to close the case before it opened.
The case was open.
